body_text:
comprises nearly a hundred and sixty species, and of the cultivated fig
there is a vast number of varieties.
According to the Yamato-honzō¹ of 1709, figs (ičijiku) were first
introduced into Nagasaki in the period Kwan-ei 寛永 (1624–44) from
the islands in the South-Western Ocean. This agrees with E. Kaem-
pfer's² statement that figs were brought into Japan and planted by
Portuguese.
footnotes: ² History of Japan, Vol. I, p. 180 (ed. reprinted Glasgow, 1906).
